square_seller_audiences.description: Audiences are the preferred method of segmenting Square Sellers for high level analytics reporting.
square_seller_audiences.uses: Use APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_MERCHANT for any merchant_token (Square Seller). Use APP_BI.HEXAGON.VDIM_USER for UNIT_TOKEN level analysis.

square_seller_sub_audiences.description: Sub Audiences are granular definitions of Audiences used for more granular reporting. Mulitple sub audiences rollup to a single audience.
square_seller_sub_audiences.uses: Use for more granular level merchant or unit token analysis.

square_gpv_segments.description: Square merchant segmentation based on 91-day rolling Gross Payment Volume (GPV) calculated in USD. These segments categorize Square sellers by their payment processing volume to enable targeted business strategies, account management, and analytics. The segmentation uses annualized GPV calculations (4 * last 91 days of GPV) and includes both primary segments and granular sub-segments for precise merchant targeting and performance analysis.

values.Inactive: Merchants with no payment processing activity ($0 GPV). These are dormant accounts that may need reactivation campaigns or account cleanup, or could be non Processing sellers like for Square Payroll
values.Micro: Small-scale merchants processing $0.01 to $125,000 in 91-day GPV. Typically individual sellers, small retailers, or service providers with limited transaction volume.
values.Micro-Low: Entry-level micro merchants processing $0.01 to $25,000 in 91-day GPV. Often new businesses or very small-scale operations.
values.Micro-Mid: Mid-tier micro merchants processing $25,000.01 to $50,000 in 91-day GPV. Established small businesses with moderate transaction volume.
values.Micro-High: High-tier micro merchants processing $50,000.01 to $125,000 in 91-day GPV. Growing small businesses approaching SMB status.
values.SMB: Small and Medium Business merchants processing $125,000.01 to $500,000 in 91-day GPV. Established businesses with regular customer flow and moderate processing needs.
values.SMB-Low: Entry-level SMB merchants processing $125,000.01 to $250,000 in 91-day GPV. Small businesses with consistent but moderate transaction volume.
values.SMB-Mid: Mid-tier SMB merchants processing $250,000.01 to $375,000 in 91-day GPV. Well-established small businesses with growing transaction volume.
values.SMB-High: High-tier SMB merchants processing $375,000.01 to $500,000 in 91-day GPV. Larger small businesses approaching mid-market status.
values.Mid-Market: Mid-market merchants processing $500,000.01 to $10,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Substantial businesses with significant transaction volumes requiring enhanced service levels.
values.MM-Low: Entry-level mid-market merchants processing $500,000.01 to $1,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Businesses transitioning from SMB to mid-market scale.
values.MM-Mid: Mid-tier mid-market merchants processing $1,000,000.01 to $5,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Established mid-market businesses with substantial processing needs.
values.MM-High: High-tier mid-market merchants processing $5,000,000.01 to $10,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Large mid-market businesses approaching enterprise status.
values.Enterprise: Enterprise-level merchants processing over $10,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Large businesses requiring premium service levels, dedicated account management, and enterprise-grade solutions.
values.Ent-Low: Entry-level enterprise merchants processing $10,000,000.01 to $20,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Businesses transitioning to enterprise-level processing volumes.
values.Ent-Mid: Mid-tier enterprise merchants processing $20,000,000.01 to $50,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Established enterprise clients with substantial processing requirements.
values.Ent-High: High-tier enterprise merchants processing over $50,000,000 in 91-day GPV. Largest Square merchants requiring premium enterprise services and support.

uses: Merchant targeting for product upselling and cross-selling campaigns
Customer support routing and service level assignment based on processing volume
Go-to-market (GTM) campaign measurement and ROI analysis across acquisition cohorts
Merchant retention analysis and churn prediction modeling
Account management assignment and resource allocation
Business intelligence reporting and merchant performance analytics
Pricing strategy development and tier-based product offerings
Market segmentation analysis for strategic planning
Revenue forecasting and growth projections by merchant segment
Product adoption analysis across different merchant sizes
Competitive analysis and market positioning by segment
Risk assessment and underwriting based on merchant processing volume

key_fields.merchant_segment: Primary segment classification (Micro, SMB, Mid-Market, Enterprise, Inactive). This is the primary field used for analytics
key_fields.merchant_sub_segment: Granular sub-segment for detailed targeting (e.g., Micro-Low, SMB-High, Ent-Mid)
key_fields.merchant_segment_91day_gpv_after_bof: Segment based on 91-day GPV calculated after Bottom of Funnel (BOF) activation date
key_fields.merchant_segment_self_reported_signup: Segment based on self-reported revenue information provided at merchant signup

important_notes: Filter by effective_end = '9999-12-31' for current active segment assignments
For historical segments (As of another date) filter to the end date of the analysis between effective_start and effective_end
Consider currency_code when analyzing cross-currency merchants
GPV thresholds are measured in gross base units (cents) and converted to USD
Segmentation uses 91-day rolling GPV calculations for dynamic merchant classification
Table includes slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking with effective begin/end dates
Self-reported segments at signup may differ from actual GPV-based segments
Inactive merchants have $0 GPV and may require reactivation strategies

source_user_type.description: The source where the user (merchant or unit) signed up. This is where the merchant originally signed up and not a designation for the type of merchant they currently are. Only use if the user asks for sign up related questions
